title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery Queen discovers that square-ended indentations in the gazebo's carpet match the marks left by the legs of a desk, which correspond to the indentations found under the sofa in Thomas Braid's study. Ellery proposes an experiment to reposition the carpet to its original location from the night of the murder to reveal a hidden bloodstain. Vane and Dr. Yadley realize that the killer, Crosetti, likely intended to delay the discovery of the true crime scene rather than hide it permanently. Ellery notes that the bloodstain is located two feet behind a folding chess chair near the desk. He argues that Crosetti did not use an ink spill to mask the blood because he intentionally left the pipe as a clue to ensure investigators would eventually return to the room.

Ellery dismisses the hypotheses that Crosetti needed to remove or add something to the room, noting that the police presence would have prevented such actions. Instead, Ellery proposes that Crosetti wanted the police to find a specific object currently in the study that was present on the night of the murder, but intended for it to be discovered only when Mr. Meagher is present. To investigate, Ellery summons the butler, Stollings, to inspect the room. Stollings confirms the room is identical to how it appeared before Braid's death, except for the missing chess table and the new bloodstain. Ellery instructs the group to split up and search for the object Crosetti intended them to find.
